key_insight: ReNIO improves on-policy distillation by upweighting negative student trajectories that better capture exploratory reasoning near the model’s capability boundary.
engineering_relevance: It offers a prefix-only training signal that boosts reasoning performance without needing full answer rollouts, making it practical for scalable LLM distillation.
method: Compute a student-to-teacher token probability ratio along generated prefixes.
Aggregate pivotal low-ratio tokens into a normalized weight for each trajectory.
Assign larger training weight to likely negative trajectories without checking final-answer correctness.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent gains over standard OPD and OPSD across mathematical reasoning and code generation, with up to 8.90% relative improvement for Qwen3-1.7B and 10.00% for R1-Distill-Qwen-7B on math benchmarks.
watch_item: The approach depends on reliable teacher-student probability ratios and may be sensitive to model calibration, teacher quality, or noisy token-level weighting.
paper_title: ReNIO: Reweighting Negative Trajectory Importance for LLM On-Policy Distillation
